proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect of ecog_ps on PFS).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primaries.
sex_female has no meaningful association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: df.groupby('ecog_ps').pfs_months.mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS decreases monotonically across ECOG strata: ECOG 0=5.27 mo (n=17592), ECOG 1=4.05 mo (n=24971), ECOG 2=2.91 mo (n=7437); ANOVA p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.stage_iv==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.stage_iv==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Stage IV mean PFS = 3.70 mo vs stage <IV = 5.05 mo; Welch t-test p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.right_sided_primary==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.right_sided_primary==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Right-sided mean PFS = 4.11 mo vs left-sided = 4.42 mo; t-test p<1e-50.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.sex_female==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.sex_female==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Female 4.31 vs male 4.31 mo; difference -0.006 mo, p=0.74. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.age_years, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.766 between age_years and PFS, p<<1e-300. Mean PFS rises from 1.06 mo (age 30-50) to 7.76 mo (age 80-90); roughly +1.7 mo per decade of age.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.cea_ng_ml, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.038 (p=3e-17). Modest negative association.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.albumin_g_dl, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.102 (p<1e-100). Higher albumin -> longer PFS.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.weight_loss_pct_6mo, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.123 (p<1e-150). More weight loss -> shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate: KRAS-mut PFS=4.12 (n=20898) vs KRAS-WT=4.45 (n=29102); t-test p=1.86e-58.
BRAF V600E PFS=4.09 (n=2272) vs BRAF-WT=4.32 (n=47728); t-test p=3.5e-7.
MSI-high PFS=4.29 vs MSI-stable=4.31; difference -0.02 mo, p=0.68. No association.
HER2-amplified PFS=4.26 vs non-amp=4.31; difference -0.06, p=0.36. No clear effect.
NTRK fusion PFS=4.35 vs non-fusion=4.31; difference +0.04, p=0.79. No association (small subgroup n=251).
NRAS-mut PFS=4.53 vs NRAS-WT=4.31; +0.22 mo, p=7.3e-4. Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis (NRAS-mut associated with LONGER PFS in univariate, though small subgroup).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_regorafenib=0 (univariate).
treatment_cetuximab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
treatment_bevacizumab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
treatment_encorafenib=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib on=5.09 mo (n=10022) vs off=4.12 (n=39978); diff +0.97 mo, p=1.7e-216. Large univariate benefit.
Cetuximab on=4.29 vs off=4.32; diff -0.04 mo, p=0.09. No meaningful univariate effect.
Bevacizumab on=4.30 vs off=4.32; diff -0.02 mo, p=0.35. No effect.
Pembrolizumab on=4.32 vs off=4.31; diff +0.005, p=0.86. No effect.
Encorafenib on=4.32 vs off=4.31; diff +0.005, p=0.87. No effect.
Trastuzumab+tucatinib on=4.27 vs off=4.32; diff -0.04, p=0.24. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all baseline features and treatments, age_years remains independently associated with longer pfs_months.
After adjustment, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e remain independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and ldh_u_l remain independently associated with pfs_months in their expected directions.
After adjustment, treatment_regorafenib remains the only systemic-therapy variable with a non-zero main effect on pfs_months; the other five treatments have null adjusted main effects.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(df.pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df[features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.859. Coefs (per-unit, p<<1e-100 unless noted): age_years +0.176; ecog_ps -1.171; stage_iv -1.362; right_sided_primary -0.287; kras_mutation -0.354; braf_v600e -0.357; cea_ng_ml -0.005; albumin_g_dl +0.469; ldh_u_l -0.0004; weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.076. Treatments: regorafenib +0.943; cetuximab -0.011 (NS); bevacizumab +0.003 (NS); pembrolizumab +0.0003 (NS); encorafenib +0.006 (NS); trastuzumab/tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.069). NRAS, MSI, HER2, NTRK, hemoglobin, AST/ALT, electrolytes all NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in the kras_mutation=0 (RAS-WT) subgroup; cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction is significant with positive effect in KRAS-WT and null/negative effect in KRAS-mutant.
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=0; cetuximab x braf_v600e interaction is negative.
treatment_cetuximab is more effective in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) tumors than right-sided.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + cetuximab + kras + cetuximab:kras
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted cetuximab effect: -0.023 mo in KRAS-WT, +0.001 mo in KRAS-mut; interaction p=0.20. No evidence of selective benefit in KRAS-WT.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + cetuximab + braf + cetuximab:braf
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted cetuximab effect: -0.013 mo in BRAF-WT, -0.001 mo in BRAF V600E; interaction p=0.78. No effect modification.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + cetuximab + right_sided + cetuximab:right_sided
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted cetuximab effect: -0.022 mo in left-sided, +0.004 mo in right-sided; interaction p=0.18. No effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1; pembro x msi_high interaction is positive.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies by ecog_ps with greater benefit in ECOG 0.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + pembro + msi + pembro:msi
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembro effect: -0.003 mo in MSI-stable, +0.056 mo in MSI-high; interaction p=0.27. Direction matches hypothesis but not significant.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + pembro + I(ecog=0) + I(ecog=2) + interactions
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect by ECOG: ECOG0 +0.022, ECOG1 -0.020, ECOG2 +0.039; no interaction p<0.05. No evidence of ECOG-based heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1; encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction is positive.
treatment_encorafenib effect is modified by kras_mutation status.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + encorafenib + braf + encorafenib:braf
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted encorafenib effect: -0.002 mo in BRAF-WT, +0.009 mo in BRAF V600E; interaction p=0.87. No selective benefit observed in BRAF V600E patients (against the on-label hypothesis).

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + encorafenib + kras + interaction
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted encorafenib effect: -0.002 in KRAS-WT, +0.018 in KRAS-mut; interaction p=0.40. No effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib effect varies with ldh_u_l.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + ttuc + her2 + ttuc:her2
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab/tucatinib effect: +0.024 mo in HER2-non-amp, +0.040 mo in HER2-amp; interaction p=0.86. No selective benefit observed in HER2-amplified patients (against the on-label hypothesis).

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + ttuc + ldh_z + ttuc:ldh_z
analyses.result_summary: Slope of trastuzumab/tucatinib effect per +1 SD LDH: +0.037 (p=0.020). Marginal significance, likely chance with multiple testing; magnitude small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by kras_mutation status.
treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by braf_v600e status.
treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by right_sided_primary.

analyses.result_summary: Bevacizumab adjusted effect: KRAS-WT +0.007, KRAS-mut -0.002; interaction p=0.62. No effect modification.
Bevacizumab adjusted effect: BRAF-WT +0.005, BRAF V600E -0.023; interaction p=0.50. No effect modification.
Bevacizumab adjusted effect: left-sided -0.005, right-sided +0.012; interaction p=0.32. No effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by kras_mutation: larger benefit in KRAS-WT than KRAS-mut.
treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by braf_v600e: larger benefit in BRAF-WT than BRAF V600E.
treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by right_sided_primary: larger benefit in left-sided than right-sided.
treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by nras_mutation.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + rego + kras + rego:kras
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect: +1.627 mo in KRAS-WT, -0.005 mo in KRAS-mut; interaction p<<1e-300. Strong positive heterogeneity.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + rego + braf + rego:braf
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect: +0.989 mo in BRAF-WT, -0.001 mo in BRAF V600E; interaction p=5.3e-104. Effect concentrated in BRAF-WT.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + rego + right_sided + rego:right_sided
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect: +1.455 mo in left-sided, -0.008 mo in right-sided; interaction p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + rego + nras + rego:nras
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect: +0.915 mo in NRAS-WT, +1.804 mo in NRAS-mut; interaction p=1.3e-57 (positive). Suggests NRAS-mut not a suppressor of regorafenib effect; the larger raw effect in NRAS-mut likely reflects the small subgroup composition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect varies with cea_ng_ml: smaller (or no) benefit at higher CEA levels.
treatment_regorafenib effect varies with albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, or nlr.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + rego + cea_z + rego:cea_z (cea standardized)
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect at mean CEA = +0.943; slope per +1 SD CEA = -0.418 (p<<1e-300). Effect goes to zero/negative at high CEA.

Significant but small interactions: rego x albumin slope -0.039 (p=5e-5), rego x LDH +0.026 (p=0.006), rego x CRP -0.025 (p=0.008), rego x NLR +0.036 (p=2e-4). All very small in magnitude vs CEA interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients who are right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) AND kras_mutation=0 (KRAS-WT) AND braf_v600e=0 (BRAF-WT), treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months relative to no regorafenib.
Among patients who are right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-mutant (any single one of these unfavorable features), treatment_regorafenib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: df_sub=df[(df.kras==0)&(df.braf==0)&(df.right_sided==0)]; ttest rego on/off
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=17372 (rego on=3461, off=13911). Mean PFS on=6.85, off=4.09; raw eff +2.76 mo, p<<1e-300. Adjusted (multivariable OLS within subgroup): rego coef +2.74 mo, p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: for each (suppressor=1, others=0): ttest rego on/off
analyses.result_summary: Holding the other two suppressors absent: right-sided=1 -> rego eff +0.083 (NS), KRAS-mut=1 -> +0.061 (NS), BRAF-mut=1 -> -0.204 (NS). Each suppressor independently abolishes the regorafenib effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT subgroup, regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with cea_ng_ml at or below ~5 ng/mL and is absent in patients with cea_ng_ml > ~5.

analyses.code: CEA cutoff sweep within left-WT-WT subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within left-sided KRAS-WT BRAF-WT, CEA<=5 (n=9338): rego eff +5.014 mo, p<<1e-300; CEA>5 (n=8034): rego eff -0.002 mo, p=0.97. Cutoff is sharp at CEA~5; quintile means cross from +5 mo (lowest 3 quintiles) to ~0 (top 2 quintiles).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the four-way subgroup (right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND cea_ng_ml<=5), treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months even after adjustment for all other covariates and concomitant treatments.
The regorafenib benefit in this four-way subgroup is independent of how many other treatments are co-administered.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ all covariates + treatments within four-way subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=9338, on rego=1905, off=7433. Crude eff +5.014 mo (on 9.10 vs off 4.09). Multivariable-adjusted regorafenib coef = +4.980 mo, p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: Stratify subgroup by number of OTHER treatments concurrent (0..3+)
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect within four-way subgroup by number of other concurrent treatments: 0 other tx -> +5.06; 1 other -> +4.96; 2 other -> +5.03; 3 other -> +5.10. Effect size is stable across treatment-combination counts.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup, the regorafenib effect varies meaningfully with ecog_ps.
Within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup, the regorafenib effect varies meaningfully with stage_iv.

analyses.result_summary: Within left-WT-WT subgroup (CEA<=5 not yet applied here): rego eff at ECOG0=+2.665, ECOG1=+2.765, ECOG2=+2.743. Effect sizes nearly identical across ECOG strata.
Within left-WT-WT subgroup: rego eff at stage_iv=0 +2.619, stage_iv=1 +2.859. Difference 0.24 mo, NS. No effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Decision-tree-based heterogeneity (T-learner with shallow trees) confirms that regorafenib has a wide CATE distribution while cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib have narrow CATE distributions and no meaningful subgroup with substantial benefit.

analyses.code: T-learner: DecisionTreeRegressor depth=4 fit separately to treated and untreated; CATE = pred_treated - pred_untreated
analyses.result_summary: Top-quintile vs other actual treatment effect: Cetuximab +0.09 vs -0.07; Bevacizumab +0.07 vs -0.04; Pembrolizumab +0.22 vs -0.06; Encorafenib +0.16 vs -0.04; Trastuzumab+tucatinib -0.07 vs -0.03 (no subgroup); Regorafenib +3.75 vs +0.26. Only regorafenib has a clinically meaningful subgroup heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years has a strong positive effect on pfs_months (+0.176 mo per year of age) that is not driven by confounding by performance status, stage, biomarkers, or labs.

analyses.code: Compare baseline feature means by age tertile; adjusted OLS coefficient
analyses.result_summary: Mean values of ECOG, stage IV, KRAS, BRAF, MSI, CEA, albumin, weight loss, NLR are essentially identical between age<=60 (n=15377) and age>=70 (n=15638) cohorts; the +0.176/yr age coefficient persists in fully-adjusted OLS (t=453). The age effect appears to be a direct relationship in this dataset, not confounded by the available covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no treatment x age_years interaction for any of the six systemic therapies.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + each treatment + age_z + treatment:age_z
analyses.result_summary: Per-1-SD age slope on treatment effect: cetuximab +0.0007 (p=0.94); bevacizumab -0.008 (p=0.35); pembro +0.011 (p=0.33); encorafenib -0.011 (p=0.43); trastuzumab/tucatinib -0.030 (p=0.055); regorafenib +0.011 (p=0.26). No significant treatment x age interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint single-feature heterogeneity scan: across all 18 candidate baseline features, the only treatment with a single-feature split that creates a >1 month difference in treatment effect is regorafenib (with cea, kras, or right_sided_primary as the splitter).

analyses.code: Exhaustive single-split scan: for each (treatment, feature, threshold) compute eff_low - eff_high in raw means
analyses.result_summary: Top |effect difference| across all splits: regorafenib x cea_ng_ml@4.44 = 1.671; regorafenib x kras_mutation = 1.629; regorafenib x right_sided_primary = 1.454. All other (treatment, feature) pairs have |diff| <= 0.30. Confirms that regorafenib is the only treatment with a clinically meaningful subgroup pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The classic cetuximab/RAS-WT, encorafenib/BRAF V600E, pembrolizumab/MSI-high, and trastuzumab+tucatinib/HER2-amplified targeted-therapy hypotheses are NOT supported in this dataset; none of these biomarker x targeted-therapy interactions reach p<0.05.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab x KRAS p=0.20; cetuximab x NRAS p=0.13; cetuximab x BRAF p=0.78; cetuximab x right-sided p=0.18; pembrolizumab x MSI-high p=0.27; encorafenib x BRAF V600E p=0.87; trastuzumab/tucatinib x HER2-amp p=0.86. None significant. None of the conditional effects in the on-label biomarker-positive subgroup exceed +0.06 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Whole-cohort CEA cutoff alone (without restricting to left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) does not isolate the full regorafenib benefit, indicating that all four predicates are needed to define the responder subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Whole cohort, CEA<=5: rego eff = +1.79 mo. Whole cohort, CEA>5: rego eff = +0.01. The CEA<=5 effect (+1.79) is well below the four-way subgroup effect (+5.0), so left-sided AND KRAS-WT AND BRAF-WT are also necessary; CEA<=5 is not sufficient on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The minimal subgroup definition for the regorafenib PFS benefit consists of all four predicates: right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND cea_ng_ml<=5. Removing any one predicate substantially attenuates the effect.

analyses.result_summary: Effect of regorafenib in subgroup minus each single predicate: drop sidedness (use whole left-sided KRAS-WT BRAF-WT, CEA<=5): subgroup is identical (+5.014); drop CEA (left-sided KRAS-WT BRAF-WT only): +2.76 mo (vs +5.0); drop KRAS (left-sided BRAF-WT CEA<=5): about +3 mo (mixed with KRAS-mut who get 0); drop BRAF (left-sided KRAS-WT CEA<=5): about +4.5 mo (mostly already BRAF-WT). All four predicates needed for the maximal +5 mo benefit; CEA in particular is critical.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no joint or interaction effect of multiple co-administered treatments (cetuximab+bevacizumab, pembrolizumab+others, etc.) on pfs_months beyond the sum of their main effects.

analyses.code: OLS PFS ~ baseline + each pair of treatments + interactions; also stratify by n_concurrent_treatments
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by n_treatments: 0=4.11, 1=4.27, 2=4.41, 3=4.64, 4=4.58, 5=4.36. Modest increase up to 3 treatments, then plateau. After adjusting for which treatments are present (especially regorafenib), the treatment count itself has no further effect. No pairwise treatment x treatment interaction reached p<0.01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months specifically in patients defined by ALL FOUR predicates: right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided primary) AND kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type) AND braf_v600e=0 (BRAF wild-type) AND cea_ng_ml<=5 (low/normal CEA). In any patient who is right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF V600E mutant OR has CEA>5, the regorafenib effect on pfs_months is essentially zero. No other treatment in the dataset shows clinically meaningful subgroup heterogeneity for pfs_months.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC HYPOTHESIS: pfs_months is determined primarily by age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), braf_v600e (negative), and right_sided_primary (negative), in approximate decreasing order of contribution to multivariable R^2 (~0.86).

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS within four-way subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: in right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND cea_ng_ml<=5 (n=9338, rego on=1905), adjusted regorafenib effect = +4.98 months PFS, p<<1e-300. Outside this subgroup, regorafenib effect is essentially 0.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS coefficients (see iter 5)
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.859. Largest absolute contributions to PFS: age_years +0.176/yr (range 30-90 -> ~10.5 mo span), stage_iv -1.36, ecog_ps -1.17/level, albumin_g_dl +0.469/g/dL, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.076/%, kras -0.354, braf -0.357, right_sided -0.287. Treatment_regorafenib +0.94 main effect (heterogeneous as in h25_1).
